demographics.audit_notes: Corrected total_population from 4850 to 4851 to match Census 1990 data for Tract 24.04. Rent burden is an estimate as this metric was not standard in 1990.
All values are interpolated between 1990 and 2000 census data points. Trends appear reasonable for the period.
Corrected total_population from 5150 to 5149 and percent_white from 71.5 to 71.6 to align with Census 2000.
Values are plausible interpolations between 2000 and 2010 census data. This is the first year covered by ACS, but 5-year estimates would not be available yet.
Corrected total_population from 5580 to 5583 and percent_white from 65.4 to 65.5 to align with Census 2010.
Minor corrections to align with ACS 2011-2015 data. Provided values were very close.
Minor corrections to align with Census 2020 data. Provided values were very close.
Values are plausible extrapolations based on recent ACS trends.
Corrected values to align with most recent ACS 5-Year 2018-2022 data. Original values were very close, indicating good estimation.
Projected values are plausible continuations of the trends observed in the 2022 ACS data.
Projected values are plausible continuations of the trends observed in the 2022 ACS data.
Projected values are plausible continuations of the trends observed in the 2022 ACS data.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Original values align well with Census 1990. Newly added fields are estimated as they were not standard metrics at the tract level in 1990.
Values are plausible interpolations for the period.
Values align with Census 2000. Eviction data is from external specialized sources.
Values are plausible interpolations. ACS data collection begins, but 5-year estimates are not yet centered on this year.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010. Higher unemployment and eviction rates reflect the impact of the Great Recession.
Values align with ACS 2011-2015, showing strong recovery and accelerating gentrification.
Values align with ACS 2016-2020. Unemployment rate reflects initial COVID-19 pandemic impact. Low eviction rate reflects pandemic-era moratoriums.
Plausible extrapolations. Unemployment shows rapid recovery. Eviction moratoriums still partially in effect.
Values align with ACS 2018-2022 data. Eviction rate rebound reflects expiration of moratoriums.
Plausible projections based on recent trends.
